metadata.title: UIAO - System Security Plan (FedRAMP Moderate Rev 5)

information-types.title: Operational Information
information-types.description: General operational data supporting UIAO TIC 3.0 mission.

authorization-boundary.description: Cloud-hosted UIAO architecture boundary spanning identity, addressing, and telemetry planes. Includes all components operating under the UIAO FedRAMP authorization.

network-architecture.description: The UIAO network architecture implements a Zero Trust model with three primary planes: Identity (Microsoft Entra ID / CyberArk), Addressing (Infoblox DDI), and Overlay/Telemetry (Cisco SD-WAN / Microsoft Sentinel). All inter-plane traffic is protected by mTLS. The architecture aligns with CISA TIC 3.0 requirements.

data-flow.description: Data flows originate at user endpoints, traverse the UIAO identity plane for authentication and authorization, and are routed through the Cisco SD-WAN overlay. All network flows are logged and aggregated in Microsoft Sentinel for continuous monitoring. No data leaves the government-only community cloud boundary without explicit authorization.

components.title: Identity Control Plane
components.description: The authoritative identity control plane for the Unified Modernization Program

components.title: Addressing Control Plane
components.description: The authoritative source of truth for identity-anchored addressing, DNS, DHCP, and cross-cloud reconciliation

components.title: Network Control Plane
components.description: A governed, identity-anchored, telemetry-driven routing and segmentation system

components.title: Telemetry Control Plane
components.description: The substrate of truth for detection, diagnosis, governance, and automation

components.title: Endpoint Control Plane
components.description: The identity-anchored, telemetry-driven governance layer for all devices

components.title: Security Control Plane
components.description: The enforcement, validation, and assurance substrate for Zero Trust across all systems, identities, networks, and workloads

props.name: subtitle
props.value: Zero Trust enforcement, validation, and assurance

control-implementation.description: Control implementations from UIAO components and compliance matrix (FedRAMP Moderate Rev 5 baseline)

values: Required for all privileged and non-privileged users
set-parameters.remarks: name: MFA Enforcement | method: Phishing-resistant (FIDO2 / PIV / CAC) | evidence_source: Microsoft Entra ID Conditional Access Policy

values: 15 minutes
set-parameters.remarks: name: Session Timeout - Privileged | scope: CyberArk PSM / PTA sessions | evidence_source: CyberArk Vault Configuration Export

values: 30 minutes
set-parameters.remarks: name: Session Timeout - Standard | scope: Entra ID token lifetime policy | evidence_source: Microsoft Entra ID Token Configuration

values: Minimum 16 characters, complexity enforced
set-parameters.remarks: name: Password Complexity | scope: All human accounts | evidence_source: Entra ID Authentication Methods Policy

values: 5 consecutive failed attempts
set-parameters.remarks: name: Account Lockout Threshold | lockout_duration: 30 minutes or admin unlock | evidence_source: Entra ID Smart Lockout Configuration

values: Microsoft Intune / Entra ID Hardware-Backed Device ID
set-parameters.remarks: name: Hardware Root of Trust - Device Identity | scope: All managed endpoints with TPM 2.0 | evidence_source: Microsoft Intune Device Compliance and Entra ID Device Registration

values: All endpoints registered in Infoblox DDI
set-parameters.remarks: name: IPAM Deterministic Assignment | scope: Enterprise network segments | evidence_source: Infoblox BloxOne DDI API

values: All DNS queries logged with source IP and timestamp
set-parameters.remarks: name: DNS Resolution Logging | retention: 365 days minimum | evidence_source: Infoblox DNS Query Log Export

values: Required on all inter-segment traffic
set-parameters.remarks: name: mTLS Enforcement | scope: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN fabric | evidence_source: Catalyst SD-WAN mTLS Policy Configuration

values: Micro-segmentation via SD-WAN policy with deny-by-default
set-parameters.remarks: name: Network Segmentation | scope: All VPN and branch connections | evidence_source: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Security Policy

values: 365 days online, 3 years archived
set-parameters.remarks: name: Audit Log Retention | scope: Microsoft Sentinel (GSA OneGov Tier - Primary AU-11) and Splunk Enterprise (Operational Forensics - Sub-Tier) | evidence_source: Microsoft Sentinel Workspace Retention Policy (GSA OneGov Tier)

values: Real-time for critical, 5-minute polling for standard
set-parameters.remarks: name: Continuous Monitoring Interval | scope: INR telemetry feed, Sentinel analytics rules | evidence_source: Microsoft INR Telemetry Configuration

values: Critical: 1 hour, High: 4 hours, Medium: 24 hours
set-parameters.remarks: name: Incident Response Time | scope: SOC triage workflow | evidence_source: Sentinel Automation Playbook SLA

values: Weekly automated scan against approved baseline
set-parameters.remarks: name: Configuration Baseline Scan | scope: All managed endpoints and servers | evidence_source: Microsoft Intune Compliance Policy

values: Authenticated scan every 72 hours
set-parameters.remarks: name: Vulnerability Scan Frequency | scope: All internet-facing and internal assets | evidence_source: ServiceNow Vulnerability Response (via Tenable scan import)

values: Monthly review cycle with 3PAO validation
set-parameters.remarks: name: Plan of Action & Milestones Review | scope: All open POA&M items | evidence_source: ServiceNow IRM POA&M Export (authoritative for PM-4)

values: All security-relevant log sources forwarded to Splunk indexers
set-parameters.remarks: name: Log Ingestion - Splunk Enterprise | scope: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud | evidence_source: Splunk Inputs.conf / HEC Configuration

values: 90 days searchable
set-parameters.remarks: name: Splunk Search Head Retention | scope: All Splunk indexes | evidence_source: Splunk Indexes.conf Retention Policy

values: Real-time EDR on all managed endpoints
set-parameters.remarks: name: Endpoint Detection - CrowdStrike Falcon | scope: CrowdStrike Falcon Sensor deployment | evidence_source: CrowdStrike Falcon Console Sensor Policy

values: Block mode enabled for malware, exploits, and lateral movement
set-parameters.remarks: name: CrowdStrike Prevention Policy | scope: All Falcon-managed hosts | evidence_source: CrowdStrike Falcon Prevention Policy Export

values: All privileged sessions brokered through CyberArk PSM with full recording
set-parameters.remarks: name: Privileged Session Management | scope: CyberArk Privilege Cloud / PAS | evidence_source: CyberArk Vault Configuration Export

values: Automated rotation every 30 days for service accounts, immediate on check-in for interactive
set-parameters.remarks: name: Credential Rotation | scope: CyberArk Central Policy Manager (CPM) | evidence_source: CyberArk CPM Rotation Logs

values: Real-time behavioral analytics on all vaulted account activity
set-parameters.remarks: name: Privileged Threat Analytics | scope: CyberArk PTA | evidence_source: CyberArk PTA Alert Feed to Splunk / Sentinel

values: Continuous ATO monitoring with automated evidence collection
set-parameters.remarks: name: GRC Platform - Continuous Authorization | scope: ServiceNow Xanadu IRM module | evidence_source: ServiceNow GRC Continuous Authorization Dashboard

values: Automated POA&M tracking with milestone validation and 3PAO integration
set-parameters.remarks: name: POA&M Lifecycle Management | scope: ServiceNow Xanadu IRM | evidence_source: ServiceNow IRM POA&M Export API

values: Third-party vendor risk assessments with continuous monitoring
set-parameters.remarks: name: Vendor Risk Management | scope: ServiceNow Xanadu VRM module | evidence_source: ServiceNow VRM Risk Register

values: Deny-by-default zone-based firewall policy on all perimeter interfaces
set-parameters.remarks: name: NGFW Boundary Protection | scope: Palo Alto NGFW managed by Panorama | evidence_source: Palo Alto Panorama Security Policy Export

values: Inline threat prevention with IPS, anti-malware, and WildFire sandbox
set-parameters.remarks: name: Threat Prevention (IPS) | scope: All Palo Alto NGFW managed interfaces | evidence_source: Palo Alto Threat Prevention Profile Configuration

values: SSL forward proxy decryption on all outbound traffic except exempted categories
set-parameters.remarks: name: SSL/TLS Decryption Policy | scope: Palo Alto NGFW decryption policy | evidence_source: Palo Alto Decryption Policy and Certificate Profile Export
